_metadata.title: Obligations for Providers of General-Purpose AI Models

primary_official_source.name: EUR-Lex -- Official Journal of the European Union

upcoming: EU AI Office Code of Practice for GPAI (published 2025-05-02)

obligations.source_quote: Providers of general-purpose AI models shall: (a) draw up and keep up-to-date the technical documentation of the model, including its training and testing process and the results of its evaluation, which shall contain, at a minimum, the information set out in Annex XI for the purpose of providing it, upon request, to the AI Office and the national competent authorities;

decomposed_atoms.description: Technical documentation of the model must exist
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL draw up technical documentation of the model

decomposed_atoms.description: Must include training process, testing process, and evaluation results
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL contain training and testing process and evaluation results

decomposed_atoms.description: Must contain at minimum the information in Annex XI
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL contain at a minimum the information set out in Annex XI

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for model cards, technical documentation, training documentation, evaluation results, and Annex XI coverage
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether documentation meets Annex XI minimum requirements

obligations.source_quote: draw up, keep up-to-date and make available information and documentation to providers of AI systems who intend to integrate the general-purpose AI model into their AI systems.

decomposed_atoms.description: Documentation for downstream AI system providers must exist
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL draw up and make available documentation to downstream providers

decomposed_atoms.description: Documentation must enable downstream providers to understand capabilities and limitations
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL enable providers of AI systems to have a good understanding of capabilities and limitations

decomposed_atoms.description: Must contain at minimum elements set out in Annex XII
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL contain at a minimum the elements set out in Annex XII

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for model cards, API documentation, integration guides, capability/limitation documentation, and Annex XII elements
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether downstream documentation is complete and enables understanding

obligations.source_quote: put in place a policy to comply with Union law on copyright and related rights, and in particular to identify and comply with, including through state-of-the-art technologies, a reservation of rights expressed pursuant to Article 4(3) of Directive (EU) 2019/790;

decomposed_atoms.description: Must have a copyright compliance policy
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL put in place a policy to comply with Union copyright law

decomposed_atoms.description: Must identify and comply with reservation of rights (opt-out)
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL identify and comply with reservation of rights per Directive 2019/790 Art. 4(3)

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for copyright policy documentation, robots.txt compliance, opt-out mechanisms, and training data documentation
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether the copyright policy is adequate and actually implemented

obligations.source_quote: draw up and make publicly available a sufficiently detailed summary about the content used for training of the general-purpose AI model, according to a template provided by the AI Office.

decomposed_atoms.description: A training data summary must exist
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL draw up a sufficiently detailed summary about training content

decomposed_atoms.description: Training data summary must be publicly available
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL make the summary publicly available

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for public training data summaries, data cards, or documentation describing training data sources

obligations.source_quote: The obligations set out in paragraph 1, points (a) and (b), shall not apply to providers of AI models that are released under a free and open-source licence that allows for the access, usage, modification, and distribution of the model, and whose parameters, including the weights, the information on the model architecture, and the information on model usage, are made publicly available. This exception shall not apply to general-purpose AI models with systemic risks.
obligations.scope_limitation: Only applies to open-source GPAI models without systemic risk

decomposed_atoms.description: Open-source GPAI providers exempt from Art. 53(1)(a) and (b) unless systemic risk
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Art. 53(1)(a)(b) SHALL NOT apply to open-source GPAI (unless systemic risk)

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check license type, model weight availability, and systemic risk classification
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether the model truly qualifies for open-source exception

obligations.source_quote: Providers of general-purpose AI models shall cooperate as necessary with the Commission and the national competent authorities in the exercise of their competences and powers pursuant to this Regulation.

decomposed_atoms.description: Must cooperate with Commission and national authorities
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL cooperate with Commission and national competent authorities

obligations.source_quote: Providers of general-purpose AI models may rely on codes of practice within the meaning of Article 56 to demonstrate compliance with the obligations set out in paragraph 1 of this Article, until a harmonised standard is published.

decomposed_atoms.description: Provider may use codes of practice to demonstrate compliance until harmonised standard exists
decomposed_atoms.requirement: MAY rely on codes of practice to demonstrate compliance until a harmonised standard is published

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for adherence to approved codes of practice or harmonised standards
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether the provider follows an approved code of practice

obligations.source_quote: Providers of general-purpose AI models who do not adhere to an approved code of practice or do not comply with a European harmonised standard shall demonstrate alternative adequate means of compliance for assessment by the Commission.
obligations.scope_limitation: Only applies to providers who do not follow an approved code of practice or harmonised standard

decomposed_atoms.description: Must demonstrate alternative adequate means of compliance if not following codes/standards
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL demonstrate alternative adequate means of compliance for assessment by the Commission

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for alternative compliance documentation submitted to Commission
